sections.paragraph:
εὐγενής, ές, gen. οῦς (s. γένος; Trag.+; loanw. in rabb.) comp. εὐγενέστερος (Mitt-Wilck. I/2 131, 33f [IV a.d.]).

① pert. to being of high status, well-born, high-born (X., Hell. 4, 1, 7; Philo, De Jos. 106; Jos., Ant. 10, 186; Just.) 1 Cor 1:26. ἄνθρωπός τις εὐ. a certain nobleman Lk 19:12.

② pert. to having the type of attitude ordinarily associated with well-bred persons, noble-minded, open-minded οὗτοι ἦσαν εὐγενέστεροι τῶν ἐν Θεσσαλονίκῃ these were more open-minded than those in Th. Ac 17:11 (cp. Menand. Dysc. 723 [FDanker, NTS 10, ’64, 366f; Cicero, Ad Att. 13, 21, 7; Jos., Ant. 12, 255).—EbNestle, ZNW 15, 1914, 91f.—DELG s.v. γίγνομαι. M-M. TW. Spicq.

gen. gen. = genitive

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Att. Att. = Attic, esp. in ref. to dialect

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 404
